sections.heading: What is Error Handling Best Practices?
sections.body: Try/catch patterns, error boundaries, user-friendly error messages. This lesson covers the essential concepts you need to understand to get started effectively.

sections.heading: Key Concepts
sections.body:
Error Handling Best Practices is a fundamental concept that every developer needs to master. Here are the core ideas:

1. Core Principle: The main idea behind Error Handling Best Practices is understanding how different components work together.

2. Application: In practice, you'll use Error Handling Best Practices every day when building applications. It's one of those skills that becomes second nature.

3. Common Patterns: Most Error Handling Best Practices implementations follow predictable patterns. Once you recognize them, you can apply them quickly.

sections.heading: Why This Matters
sections.body: Understanding Error Handling Best Practices is crucial because it builds the foundation for everything that follows. Master this topic and you'll find subsequent concepts much easier to learn.

summary: Error Handling Best Practices is a core concept in reliability. Try/catch patterns, error boundaries, user-friendly error messages. Focus on understanding the fundamentals first, then practice applying them.
